summary: Dependency-confusion package exfiltrates installer hostname and username on install
details: @orion-design-system/foundation@9999.0.2 is a dependency-confusion package squatting an internal-looking scope at an inflated version (9999.0.2) so it wins npm resolution against private registry versions. Its package.json declares a preinstall script that runs `node -e` to require `https` and `os`, reads `os.hostname()` and `os.userInfo().username`, and sends them via HTTPS GET to `https://bzexvuarrenmkxiyjrichx8eaxwssgv0x.oast.fun/?h=<hostname>&u=<username>` (an interactsh/OAST out-of-band callback domain). This fires automatically on `npm install` without user consent. Any build pipeline that resolves this package leaks installer host identifiers to a third party. The README's `authorized security research` framing does not change installer-side impact: hostnames and usernames of every install machine are emitted to an attacker-controlled (or researcher-controlled, but uncontrolled-by-installer) external listener.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
